outlook.headline: HBM4 IP still wins, but momentum is fading
outlook.summary: Fundamentally, HBM4 IP still shows real traction: Micron is shipping/ramping HBM4/HBM4E into AI platforms, Synopsys delivered the first HBM4 IP test chip, and Rambus has the fastest HBM4E controller. But mention momentum has rolled over to 0.2x and sits 20% below its trailing average, so the next 1-2 quarters look more like a selective leader story than a broadening cycle; watch whether adoption extends beyond a few flagship AI platforms.

drivers.label: AI platform ramp for HBM4/HBM4E
drivers.detail: HBM4 and HBM4E are shipping or ramping into AI platforms, supporting near-term traction and mix control.

drivers.label: First HBM4 IP test chip milestone
drivers.detail: Industry-first HBM4 IP test chip points to leading-edge design engagement in the segment.

drivers.label: Fastest HBM4E controller
drivers.detail: The fastest HBM4E controller suggests strong demand for advanced memory IP in AI accelerators.

drivers.label: Higher-speed stack testing demand
drivers.detail: HBM4/HBM5 progression and SmartMatrix’s ability to test more stacks at higher speeds support ongoing demand.

drivers.label: Mention momentum deterioration
drivers.detail: Topic mentions are down to 0.2x, with momentum 20% below the trailing average, indicating weaker breadth in the conversation.

risks: Adoption remains concentrated in a few AI platforms rather than broadening across the market.
HBM4E/HBM5 progression could shift attention away from HBM4 IP sooner than expected.
Weakening mention momentum could persist even if underlying product traction remains solid.
